(Carl Gustaf Folke Hubertus; born 30 April 1946) is [King of Sweden](/wiki/King_of_Sweden "King of Sweden"), reigning since 1973. Having reigned for 52 years, he is the longest-reigning monarch in [Swedish history](/wiki/History_of_Sweden "History of Sweden").

**Carl XVI Gustaf** (Carl Gustaf Folke Hubertus; born 30 April 1946) is [King of Sweden](/wiki/King_of_Sweden "King of Sweden"), reigning since 1973. Having reigned for 52 years, he is the longest-reigning monarch in [Swedish history](/wiki/History_of_Sweden "History of Sweden").

Carl Gustaf was born during the reign of his paternal great-grandfather, [King Gustaf V](/wiki/King_Gustaf_V "King Gustaf V"), as the youngest child and only son of [Prince Gustaf Adolf, Duke of Västerbotten](/wiki/Prince_Gustaf_Adolf,_Duke_of_V%C3%A4sterbotten "Prince Gustaf Adolf, Duke of Västerbotten"), and [Princess Sibylla of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/Princess_Sibylla_of_Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "Princess Sibylla of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha"). His father died [in an airplane crash](/wiki/1947_KLM_Douglas_DC-3_crash "1947 KLM Douglas DC-3 crash") in Denmark in January 1947, when Carl Gustaf was nine months old. Carl Gustaf became crown prince and [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent") to the Swedish throne at the age of four when his grandfather [Gustaf VI Adolf](/wiki/Gustaf_VI_Adolf "Gustaf VI Adolf") acceded to the throne in 1950.

Carl Gustaf acceded to the throne upon his grandfather's death on 15 September 1973. Shortly after he became king, the new [1974 Instrument of Government](/wiki/Basic_Laws_of_Sweden#Instrument_of_Government "Basic Laws of Sweden") took effect, formally stripping the monarchy of its remaining executive powers. As a result, Carl Gustaf no longer performs many of the duties normally accorded to a [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") in parliamentary regimes, such as the formal appointment of the prime minister, signing legislation into law, and being [commander-in-chief](/wiki/Commander-in-chief "Commander-in-chief") of the nation's military. The new instrument explicitly limited the king to ceremonial and representative functions, while he retained the right to be regularly informed of affairs of state. As head of the [House of Bernadotte](/wiki/House_of_Bernadotte "House of Bernadotte"), Carl Gustaf has also been able to make a number of decisions about the titles and positions of its members.

In June 1976, [Carl Gustaf married](/wiki/Wedding_of_Carl_XVI_Gustaf_and_Silvia_Sommerlath "Wedding of Carl XVI Gustaf and Silvia Sommerlath") [Silvia Sommerlath](/wiki/Queen_Silvia_of_Sweden "Queen Silvia of Sweden"). They have three children: [Victoria](/wiki/Victoria,_Crown_Princess_of_Sweden "Victoria, Crown Princess of Sweden"), [Carl Philip](/wiki/Prince_Carl_Philip,_Duke_of_V%C3%A4rmland "Prince Carl Philip, Duke of Värmland"), and [Madeleine](/wiki/Princess_Madeleine,_Duchess_of_H%C3%A4lsingland_and_G%C3%A4strikland "Princess Madeleine, Duchess of Hälsingland and Gästrikland"). The king's [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent"), after passage on 1 January 1980 of a new law establishing [absolute primogeniture](/wiki/Absolute_primogeniture "Absolute primogeniture"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) is his eldest child, Crown Princess Victoria. Victoria's younger brother, Carl Philip, was briefly the heir apparent from his birth in May 1979 until the application of said law.

## Early life

Carl Gustaf was born on 30 April 1946 at 10:20[[3]](#cite_note-3) in [Haga Palace](/wiki/Haga_Palace "Haga Palace") in [Solna](/wiki/Solna "Solna"), [Stockholm County](/wiki/Stockholm_County "Stockholm County"). He was the youngest of five children and the only son of Sweden's Prince Gustaf Adolf and Princess Sibylla. He was christened at the [Royal Chapel](/wiki/Royal_Chapel_(Sweden) "Royal Chapel (Sweden)") on 7 June 1946 by the [Archbishop of Uppsala](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Uppsala "Archbishop of Uppsala"), [Erling Eidem](/wiki/Erling_Eidem "Erling Eidem").[[4]](#cite_note-Lundgren_Larsson_Jaeger_Engman_2017_p._22-IA52-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Gustaf_Adolf_%26_Sibylla.jpg/250px-Gustaf_Adolf_%26_Sibylla.jpg)](/wiki/File:Gustaf_Adolf_%26_Sibylla.jpg)

Carl Gustaf's parents, Prince Gustaf Adolf and Princess Sibylla

Carl Gustaf was baptised in [Charles XI](/wiki/Charles_XI "Charles XI")'s baptismal font, which stood on [Gustav III](/wiki/Gustav_III "Gustav III")'s carpet; he lay in Charles XI's cradle with [Oscar II](/wiki/Oscar_II "Oscar II")'s crown beside him.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The christening gown in white linen [batiste](/wiki/Batiste "Batiste") that the prince wore had been worn by his father in 1906 and would later be worn by his three children.[[6]](#cite_note-6) His godparents were the [Crown Prince](/wiki/Frederick_IX_of_Denmark "Frederick IX of Denmark") and [Crown Princess of Denmark](/wiki/Ingrid_of_Sweden "Ingrid of Sweden") (his paternal uncle and aunt), the [Crown Prince of Norway](/wiki/Olav_V_of_Norway "Olav V of Norway"), [Princess Juliana of the Netherlands](/wiki/Princess_Juliana_of_the_Netherlands "Princess Juliana of the Netherlands"), the [King of Sweden](/wiki/Gustaf_V_of_Sweden "Gustaf V of Sweden") (his paternal great-grandfather), the [Hereditary Prince of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/Friedrich_Josias,_Prince_of_Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "Friedrich Josias, Prince of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha") (his maternal uncle), the [Crown Prince](/wiki/Gustaf_VI_Adolf_of_Sweden "Gustaf VI Adolf of Sweden") and [Crown Princess of Sweden](/wiki/Louise_Mountbatten "Louise Mountbatten") (his paternal grandfather and step-grandmother), and [Count Folke](/wiki/Folke_Bernadotte "Folke Bernadotte") and Countess Maria Bernadotte of [Wisborg](/wiki/Wisborg "Wisborg").[[7]](#cite_note-kungens_liv-7)

Prince Carl Gustaf was also given the title of the Duke of [Jämtland](/wiki/J%C3%A4mtland "Jämtland"). His father, [Prince Gustaf Adolf, Duke of Västerbotten](/wiki/Prince_Gustaf_Adolf,_Duke_of_V%C3%A4sterbotten "Prince Gustaf Adolf, Duke of Västerbotten"), was killed in an [airplane crash](/wiki/1947_KLM_Douglas_DC-3_Copenhagen_accident "1947 KLM Douglas DC-3 Copenhagen accident") on 26 January 1947 at [Copenhagen Airport](/wiki/Copenhagen_Airport "Copenhagen Airport"). His father's death had left the nine-month-old prince second in line for the throne, behind his grandfather, then Crown Prince Gustaf Adolf. When his paternal great-grandfather, [Gustaf V](/wiki/Gustaf_V "Gustaf V") died in 1950, the four-year-old prince became the [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent") of Sweden.[[7]](#cite_note-kungens_liv-7)

Carl Gustaf was seven years old before he was told about his father's death. He expressed his feelings about growing up without knowing his father in a speech in 2005.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Youth and education

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Vasa_Carl_Gustaf_1961.jpg/250px-Vasa_Carl_Gustaf_1961.jpg)](/wiki/File:Vasa_Carl_Gustaf_1961.jpg)

The 15-year-old Crown Prince of Sweden looks at the recently recovered 17th-century warship *[Vasa](/wiki/Vasa_(ship) "Vasa (ship)")* in 1961.

Carl Gustaf's earliest education was received privately at the [Royal Palace](/wiki/Stockholm_Palace "Stockholm Palace"). He was then sent to [Broms school](/w/index.php?title=Broms_school&action=edit&redlink=1 "Broms school (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broms_skola "sv:Broms skola")], and then on to [Sigtuna boarding school](/wiki/Sigtunaskolan_Humanistiska_L%C3%A4roverket "Sigtunaskolan Humanistiska Läroverket"). After graduating from [high school](/wiki/High_school_(upper_secondary) "High school (upper secondary)") in 1966, Carl Gustaf completed two-and-a-half years of education in the [Swedish Army](/wiki/Swedish_Army "Swedish Army"), the [Royal Swedish Navy](/wiki/Royal_Swedish_Navy "Royal Swedish Navy"), and the [Swedish Air Force](/wiki/Swedish_Air_Force "Swedish Air Force"). During the winter of 1966–67, he took part in a round-the-world voyage with the [mine-laying vessel](/wiki/Mine_layer "Mine layer") *[Älvsnabben](/wiki/HSwMS_%C3%84lvsnabben_(M01) "HSwMS Älvsnabben (M01)")*. Carl Gustaf received his commission as an officer in all three services in 1968, eventually rising to the rank of [captain](/wiki/Captain "Captain") (in the army and air force) and [lieutenant](/wiki/Lieutenant "Lieutenant") (in the navy), before his accession to the throne. He also completed his academic studies in [history](/wiki/History "History"), [sociology](/wiki/Sociology "Sociology"), [political science](/wiki/Political_science "Political science"), [tax law](/wiki/Tax_law "Tax law"), and [economics](/wiki/Economics "Economics") at [Uppsala University](/wiki/Uppsala_University "Uppsala University") and later economics at [Stockholm University](/wiki/Stockholm_University "Stockholm University").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

To prepare for his role as [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state"), Carl Gustaf followed a broad program of studies on the court system, social organisations and institutions, [trade unions](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union"), and employers' associations. In addition, he closely studied the affairs of the [Riksdag](/wiki/Riksdag "Riksdag"), Government, and [Ministry for Foreign Affairs](/wiki/Ministry_for_Foreign_Affairs_(Sweden) "Ministry for Foreign Affairs (Sweden)"). He also spent time at the Swedish mission to the [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") and the [Swedish International Development Cooperation Agency](/wiki/Swedish_International_Development_Cooperation_Agency "Swedish International Development Cooperation Agency") (SIDA), worked at a bank and the Swedish embassy in London, and at the Swedish Chamber of Commerce and at the [Alfa Laval](/wiki/Alfa_Laval "Alfa Laval") Company factory in France. In 1970, he represented King Gustaf VI Adolf at the head of the Swedish delegation to the [World Exposition](/wiki/World_Exposition "World Exposition") in [Osaka](/wiki/Osaka "Osaka"), Japan. Since his youth, Carl Gustaf has been a strong supporter of the [Scout Movement](/wiki/Scout_Movement "Scout Movement") in Sweden.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Carl Gustaf has [dyslexia](/wiki/Dyslexia "Dyslexia"), as do his daughter Crown Princess Victoria and his son Prince Carl Philip.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) He holds [honorary](/wiki/Honorary_degree "Honorary degree") doctoral degrees from the [Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences](/wiki/Swedish_University_of_Agricultural_Sciences "Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences"), [KTH Royal Institute of Technology](/wiki/KTH_Royal_Institute_of_Technology "KTH Royal Institute of Technology"), the [Stockholm School of Economics](/wiki/Stockholm_School_of_Economics "Stockholm School of Economics") and from the [Åbo Akademi University](/wiki/%C3%85bo_Akademi_University "Åbo Akademi University") in Finland.

## Reign

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden_%26_Stig_Stefanson_1975.jpg/250px-Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden_%26_Stig_Stefanson_1975.jpg)](/wiki/File:Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden_%26_Stig_Stefanson_1975.jpg)

Carl Gustaf and [MP](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament "Member of Parliament") [Stig Stefanson](/w/index.php?title=Stig_Stefanson&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stig Stefanson (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stig_Stefanson "sv:Stig Stefanson")] at [Stockholm City Hall](/wiki/Stockholm_City_Hall "Stockholm City Hall") in 1975

Carl Gustaf ascended the throne upon the death of his grandfather, Gustaf VI Adolf, on 15 September 1973. Four days later, he took the required regal assurance ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Konungaförsäkran*) during an extraordinary meeting of the cabinet. Afterwards, he appeared before members of parliament, the diplomatic corps and court in the Hall of State at the Royal Palace where he was enthroned on [the Silver Throne](/wiki/Silver_Throne "Silver Throne") and gave a speech. Both the cabinet meeting and ceremony at the Hall were broadcast live on television. Following the ceremonies, he appeared on the balcony to acknowledge gathered crowds.[[13]](#cite_note-:2-13)

Carl Gustaf undertook his first state visit as king on 6 October 1974, meeting with his godfather King [Olav V](/wiki/Olav_V "Olav V") in Norway.[[14]](#cite_note-:3-14)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Kungafamiljen_%26_prinsessan_Estelle_den_30_april_2012..JPG/250px-Kungafamiljen_%26_prinsessan_Estelle_den_30_april_2012..JPG)](/wiki/File:Kungafamiljen_%26_prinsessan_Estelle_den_30_april_2012..JPG)

The royal family appearing on the balcony of the Royal Palace for the king's 66th birthday, 30 April 2012

As head of state, he is the foremost representative of Sweden and pays [state visits abroad](/wiki/List_of_state_visits_made_by_Carl_XVI_Gustaf "List of state visits made by Carl XVI Gustaf") and [receives](/wiki/List_of_state_visits_received_by_Carl_XVI_Gustaf "List of state visits received by Carl XVI Gustaf") those to Sweden; he [opens the annual session](/wiki/Opening_of_the_Riksdag "Opening of the Riksdag") of the [Riksdag](/wiki/Riksdag "Riksdag"), chairs the [Special Council held during a change of Government](/wiki/Council_of_State_(Sweden) "Council of State (Sweden)") (*skifteskonselj*), holds regular Information Councils with the Prime Minister and the Cabinet (*informationskonselj*), chairs the meetings of the [Advisory Council on Foreign Affairs](/wiki/Advisory_Council_on_Foreign_Affairs "Advisory Council on Foreign Affairs") (*utrikesnämnden*), receives [letters of credence](/wiki/Letter_of_credence "Letter of credence") of foreign ambassadors to Sweden and signs those of Sweden to foreign nations, and annually presents the [Nobel Prizes](/wiki/Nobel_Prize "Nobel Prize")[[a]](#cite_note-16) and the [Polar Music Prize](/wiki/Polar_Music_Prize "Polar Music Prize"). As a figurehead, he also voluntarily abstains from voting in [Swedish elections](/wiki/Swedish_elections "Swedish elections").[[16]](#cite_note-17)

Carl Gustaf holds the highest ranks in the three branches of the [Swedish Armed Forces](/wiki/Swedish_Armed_Forces "Swedish Armed Forces"); this is due to the fact that he was, as stipulated by § 14 of the [1809 Instrument of Government](/wiki/1809_Instrument_of_Government "1809 Instrument of Government") in effect at the time of his accession to the throne in 1973, the [Commander-in-Chief](/wiki/Commander-in-chief#Sweden "Commander-in-chief") ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Högste Befälhavare*; not to be confused with the military professional holding the position of [Supreme Commander](/wiki/Supreme_Commander_of_the_Swedish_Armed_Forces "Supreme Commander of the Swedish Armed Forces")) and therefore he was promoted *[ex officio](/wiki/Ex_officio "Ex officio")* from his earlier ranks of captain (Army & Air Force) and lieutenant (Navy), to [general](/wiki/General_(Sweden) "General (Sweden)") and [admiral](/wiki/Admiral_(Sweden) "Admiral (Sweden)"). Under the provisions of the [Instrument of Government of 1974](/wiki/Constitution_of_Sweden "Constitution of Sweden"), which became effective on 1 January 1975, the King no longer holds this constitutionally-mandated position, but he kept his ranks *[à la suite](/wiki/%C3%80_la_suite "À la suite")* since he no longer has any military command authority, except over [His Majesty's Military Staff](/wiki/His_Majesty%27s_Military_Staff "His Majesty's Military Staff").

On 26 April 2018, Carl Gustaf became the longest-reigning Swedish monarch when he surpassed [Magnus Eriksson](/wiki/Magnus_Eriksson "Magnus Eriksson")'s reign of 44 years and 222 days.[[17]](#cite_note-18)

[Carl Gustaf's Golden Jubilee](/wiki/Golden_Jubilee_of_Carl_XVI_Gustaf "Golden Jubilee of Carl XVI Gustaf") was celebrated in 2023. The celebrations included tours of all of Sweden's 21 counties, a jubilee banquet at the Royal Palace and a carriage procession through the streets of Stockholm. Leading up to his jubilee and beginning in 2018, Carl Gustaf and the way his monarchy has developed saw a rise in criticism being published.[[18]](#cite_note-19)[[19]](#cite_note-20)[[20]](#cite_note-21)[[21]](#cite_note-22) Support for the monarchy overall remained strong in the Swedish public, however, in large part due to the popularity of [Crown Princess Victoria](/wiki/Crown_Princess_Victoria "Crown Princess Victoria").[[22]](#cite_note-23)

Following the [abdication](/wiki/Abdication_of_Margrethe_II "Abdication of Margrethe II") of his cousin, [Margrethe II](/wiki/Margrethe_II "Margrethe II") of Denmark, Carl Gustaf became the longest-serving incumbent head of state in Europe.[[23]](#cite_note-24) His reign saw Sweden end over 200 years of neutrality by joining [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") on 18 March 2024.[[24]](#cite_note-:0-25)[[25]](#cite_note-:1-26)

## Views

Carl Gustaf has made a number of controversial statements considered political. In 1989, he criticised Norway's [seal hunting](/wiki/Seal_hunting "Seal hunting") policy, saying that "if [prime minister] [Gro Harlem Brundtland](/wiki/Gro_Harlem_Brundtland "Gro Harlem Brundtland") cannot take care of the seal problems, I wonder how she will be able to take care of the Norwegian people".[[26]](#cite_note-27)[[27]](#cite_note-28) In 2004, after a state visit to [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"), he praised Sultan [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") and described Brunei as an "open country", despite its controversial [human rights record](/wiki/Human_rights_in_Brunei "Human rights in Brunei").[[28]](#cite_note-29) In 2023 Carl Gustaf said that while he understands that Brunei has a non-democratic form of government, it is still an open country.[[29]](#cite_note-30)

Following the [2004 Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami](/wiki/2004_Indian_Ocean_earthquake_and_tsunami "2004 Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami"), in which many Swedes died, then-prime minister [Göran Persson](/wiki/G%C3%B6ran_Persson "Göran Persson") had failed to carry out his constitutional obligation to inform the king on matters of state, resulting in criticism of his government. During a memorial ceremony held at [Stockholm City Hall](/wiki/Stockholm_City_Hall "Stockholm City Hall") on 10 January 2005 the king gave a highly praised speech which restored support of the monarchy.[[30]](#cite_note-31)[[31]](#cite_note-32)

In 2015, Carl Gustaf offered to assist in resolving a diplomatic crisis between Sweden and [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"), which began when foreign minister [Margot Wallström](/wiki/Margot_Wallstr%C3%B6m "Margot Wallström") criticized Saudi Arabia's form of government and human rights situation. Saudi Arabia responded by recalling its ambassador to Sweden and ending a military co-operation agreement between the two nations. The [Swedish government](/wiki/L%C3%B6fven_I_cabinet "Löfven I cabinet") then reportedly asked Carl Gustaf to write a letter to the [Saudi Arabian king](/wiki/Salman_of_Saudi_Arabia "Salman of Saudi Arabia"), which ended the crisis.[[32]](#cite_note-33)[[33]](#cite_note-34) In 2016, Carl Gustaf said that the letter he wrote played a role in resolving the dispute with Saudi Arabia and added that he had "good relations" with the Saudi king, which led to criticism.[[34]](#cite_note-35)[[35]](#cite_note-36)[[36]](#cite_note-37)

In 2016, Carl Gustaf intervened in a debate surrounding the proposed Nobel Center at [Blasieholmen](/wiki/Blasieholmen "Blasieholmen") in central Stockholm, near the [Nationalmuseum](/wiki/Nationalmuseum "Nationalmuseum") and old town, saying that the proposed structure was "too big and in the wrong place" and that it "could be relocated".[[37]](#cite_note-38) Following the [2018 election](/wiki/2018_Swedish_general_election "2018 Swedish general election"), the City of Stockholm abandoned the original proposal, opting instead for creating new plans near [Slussen](/wiki/Slussen "Slussen").[[38]](#cite_note-39)[[39]](#cite_note-40)

In December 2020, Carl Gustaf said that Sweden "failed" to save lives with its approach to dealing with the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Sweden "COVID-19 pandemic in Sweden"), which involved not imposing a full national [lockdown](/wiki/COVID-19_lockdowns "COVID-19 lockdowns").[[40]](#cite_note-41)

In March 2022, the King condemned the [Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "Russian invasion of Ukraine") during a visit to the Life Regiment Hussars to present a new standard to the regiment. He stated that Europe was in an extremely difficult situation and accused Russia of violating international law and creating a humanitarian catastrophe.[[41]](#cite_note-42)[[42]](#cite_note-43)

In 2023, the [Nobel Foundation](/wiki/Nobel_Foundation "Nobel Foundation") announced that they intended to invite ambassadors from [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus") and [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran") to attend that year's Nobel Prize awards ceremony. This sparked mass criticism and the royal court issued a statement saying the king was still deciding on whether or not to attend the ceremony, as has been tradition since it was first held. Many leaders of political parties also threatened to boycott the ceremony.[[43]](#cite_note-44)[[44]](#cite_note-45) Eventually the Foundation backed down on its decision.[[45]](#cite_note-46)

During a ceremony held outside the [Riksdag building](/wiki/Riksdag_building "Riksdag building") to mark Sweden's accession to NATO in 2024, Carl Gustaf described the move to join the alliance as a new era in Swedish security policy and reaffirmed Sweden's wish for peace.[[24]](#cite_note-:0-25)[[25]](#cite_note-:1-26) At the 2025 [Society and Defence National Conference](/wiki/Society_and_Defence "Society and Defence") in Sälen, Carl Gustaf addressed Sweden's security situation. He said that whilst Sweden was not at war it could no longer consider itself at peace, echoing a sentiment voiced by prime minister [Ulf Kristersson](/wiki/Ulf_Kristersson "Ulf Kristersson"). He also highlighted the need societal preparedness and stated that he believed the public's willingness to defend the realm had grown in recent years.[[46]](#cite_note-47)[[47]](#cite_note-48)

## Personal life

### Personal interests

Carl Gustaf is passionate about the environment, technology, agriculture, trade, and industry. Like many members of the Swedish royal family, he has a keen interest in automobiles. He owns several [Porsche 911s](/wiki/Porsche_911 "Porsche 911"), a car model which is said to be a particular favourite of his, as well as a vintage [Volvo PV444](/wiki/Volvo_PV444 "Volvo PV444"), a [Ferrari 456](/wiki/Ferrari_456 "Ferrari 456")M GT, an [AC Cobra](/wiki/AC_Cobra "AC Cobra") and other cars.[[48]](#cite_note-49) The first pictures taken of him and his future wife were of them sitting in his Porsche 911. In the summer of 2005, he was involved in a traffic accident in [Norrköping](/wiki/Norrk%C3%B6ping "Norrköping"), which was described as a "fender bender", with no serious personal injuries. Nevertheless, the incident caused national headlines.[[49]](#cite_note-50) Carl Gustaf and Silvia have attended several Summer and Winter [Olympic Games](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games").[[50]](#cite_note-51)[[51]](#cite_note-52)[[52]](#cite_note-53)

#### Scouting

Carl Gustaf is the honorary chairman of the [World Scout Foundation](/wiki/World_Scout_Foundation "World Scout Foundation"), and often participates in [scout](/wiki/Scouting "Scouting") activities both [in Sweden](/wiki/Svenska_Scoutr%C3%A5det "Svenska Scoutrådet") and abroad. He regularly visits [World Scout Jamborees](/wiki/World_Scout_Jamboree "World Scout Jamboree"), for instance the 1979 [Dalajamb World Jamboree International Encampment](/wiki/15th_World_Scout_Jamboree_(cancelled)#World_Jamboree_Year "15th World Scout Jamboree (cancelled)") hosted by Sweden, the 2002 World Jamboree held in [Sattahip, Thailand](/wiki/20th_World_Scout_Jamboree "20th World Scout Jamboree"), and the 100th Anniversary of World Scouting 2007 World Jamboree held [in Hylands Park, England](/wiki/21st_World_Scout_Jamboree "21st World Scout Jamboree").[[53]](#cite_note-54) He also attended the [1981 National Scout Jamboree](/wiki/National_Scout_jamboree_(Boy_Scouts_of_America) "National Scout jamboree (Boy Scouts of America)") in [Virginia](/wiki/Virginia "Virginia"), United States, and was awarded the [Bronze Wolf](/wiki/Bronze_Wolf "Bronze Wolf"), the only distinction of the [World Organization of the Scout Movement](/wiki/World_Organization_of_the_Scout_Movement "World Organization of the Scout Movement"), awarded by the World Scout Committee for exceptional services to world Scouting, in 1982. He also attended the [22nd World Scout Jamboree](/wiki/22nd_World_Scout_Jamboree "22nd World Scout Jamboree"). He gave a speech on 6 August 2011 at the closing ceremony with more than 40,000 people watching. The band [Europe](/wiki/Europe_(band) "Europe (band)") also performed for him singing "The Final Countdown". King Carl Gustaf made an appearance at the 2013 Boy Scouts of America National Jamboree in West Virginia.[[54]](#cite_note-55)

### Marriage and family

Main article: [Wedding of Carl XVI Gustaf and Silvia Sommerlath](/wiki/Wedding_of_Carl_XVI_Gustaf_and_Silvia_Sommerlath "Wedding of Carl XVI Gustaf and Silvia Sommerlath")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0f/King_and_Queen_of_Sweden.jpg/250px-King_and_Queen_of_Sweden.jpg)](/wiki/File:King_and_Queen_of_Sweden.jpg)

King Carl XVI Gustaf with Queen Silvia at the royal wedding of their daughter Victoria

Carl Gustaf met [Silvia Sommerlath](/wiki/Silvia_Sommerlath "Silvia Sommerlath"), an interpreter and host born to a German father and a Brazilian mother, at the [1972 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1972_Summer_Olympics "1972 Summer Olympics") in Munich. They married on 19 June 1976, at [Stockholm Cathedral](/wiki/Stockholm_Cathedral "Stockholm Cathedral"), in a ceremony officiated by the [Archbishop of Uppsala](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Uppsala "Archbishop of Uppsala"), Olof Sundby.[[55]](#cite_note-56) The wedding was preceded the previous evening by a Royal Variety Performance, at which, among other performances, the Swedish musical group [ABBA](/wiki/ABBA "ABBA") gave one of the first performances of "[Dancing Queen](/wiki/Dancing_Queen "Dancing Queen")", as a tribute to Sweden's future queen.[[56]](#cite_note-57)

Carl Gustaf and Silvia's first joint state visit was to the Netherlands, on 25 October 1976.[[14]](#cite_note-:3-14) In 1980, Carl Gustaf and his family moved to [Drottningholm Palace](/wiki/Drottningholm_Palace "Drottningholm Palace") west of Stockholm, although they continue to perform their official working duties at the Royal Palace of Stockholm.

King Carl Gustaf and Queen Silvia have three children and nine grandchildren:

* [Crown Princess Victoria, Duchess of Västergötland](/wiki/Crown_Princess_Victoria,_Duchess_of_V%C3%A4sterg%C3%B6tland "Crown Princess Victoria, Duchess of Västergötland") (born 14 July 1977), who is married to [Daniel Westling](/wiki/Daniel_Westling "Daniel Westling") and has two children
* [Prince Carl Philip, Duke of Värmland](/wiki/Prince_Carl_Philip,_Duke_of_V%C3%A4rmland "Prince Carl Philip, Duke of Värmland") (born 13 May 1979), who is married to [Sofia Hellqvist](/wiki/Sofia_Hellqvist "Sofia Hellqvist") and has four children
* [Princess Madeleine, Duchess of Hälsingland and Gästrikland](/wiki/Princess_Madeleine,_Duchess_of_H%C3%A4lsingland_and_G%C3%A4strikland "Princess Madeleine, Duchess of Hälsingland and Gästrikland") (born 10 June 1982), who is married to [Christopher O'Neill](/wiki/Christopher_O%27Neill "Christopher O'Neill") and has three children

Prince Carl Philip was born the [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent"). However, a constitutional reform, which was already underway at the time of his birth, made his elder sister, Victoria, the heir apparent and Crown Princess of Sweden when it took effect on 1 January 1980, making Sweden the first monarchy to adopt [absolute primogeniture](/wiki/Absolute_primogeniture "Absolute primogeniture") for its [line of succession](/wiki/Succession_to_the_Swedish_throne "Succession to the Swedish throne").[[57]](#cite_note-58) Carl Gustaf expressed criticism at his son losing the position and title which he had from birth as a result of the adoption of said law.[[58]](#cite_note-59)

### Health

In February 2023, Carl Gustaf underwent "a surgical intervention with [catheter](/wiki/Catheter "Catheter") technology in the heart area."[[59]](#cite_note-60)

## Use of remaining power

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/34/Sveriges_Nationaldag_2013-4.jpg/250px-Sveriges_Nationaldag_2013-4.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sveriges_Nationaldag_2013-4.jpg)

Carl Gustaf created a new Swedish double duchy for his daughter [Princess Madeleine](/wiki/Princess_Madeleine,_Duchess_of_H%C3%A4lsingland_and_G%C3%A4strikland "Princess Madeleine, Duchess of Hälsingland and Gästrikland") (left) in 1982, whereas [her husband](/wiki/Christopher_O%27Neill "Christopher O'Neill") in 2013 declined the king's offer to become a Swedish prince and duke, and is styled *[Herr](/wiki/Herr_(title) "Herr (title)")* Christopher O'Neill in Sweden.

So empowered as head of the [House of Bernadotte](/wiki/House_of_Bernadotte "House of Bernadotte"),[[60]](#cite_note-61) King Carl Gustaf, since he was enthroned in 1973, has made a number of personal decisions regarding the titles and positions of relatives and family members, including the demotion of a sister, elevation of several [commoners](/wiki/Commoner "Commoner") to royalty, rebuff of an elderly uncle's wishes and the creation of new Swedish titles and duchies.

* 1974: his sister [Christina](/wiki/Princess_Christina,_Mrs._Magnuson "Princess Christina, Mrs. Magnuson") married a non-royal Swedish man and Carl Gustaf followed the example which his grandfather and predecessor had set for two of Christina's older sisters with like marriages, so Christina was removed from the [Royal House](/wiki/Swedish_royal_family#Royal_House "Swedish royal family"), no longer a *Royal Highness* and was given the courtesy title *Princess Christina, Mrs. Magnuson* (a special non-royal, non-noble style first invented in 1953 by [King Haakon VII of Norway](/wiki/King_Haakon_VII_of_Norway "King Haakon VII of Norway") for his granddaughter [Ragnhild](/wiki/Princess_Ragnhild,_Mrs._Lorentzen "Princess Ragnhild, Mrs. Lorentzen")).
* 1976: his own choice, taking advantage of his constitutional prerogative as king when he married a non-royal German-Brazilian woman, saw her created *Her Majesty Queen Silvia of Sweden*.
* 1976: his paternal uncle [Bertil](/wiki/Prince_Bertil,_Duke_of_Halland "Prince Bertil, Duke of Halland") (later that year) married the non-royal British woman who had lived with Bertil for decades, and (with Bertil's titles) Carl Gustaf created her a *Royal Highness* Princess of Sweden and Duchess of Halland.
* 1977: his daughter [Victoria](/wiki/Victoria,_Crown_Princess_of_Sweden "Victoria, Crown Princess of Sweden") was born, and in 1980, Carl Gustaf created her Duchess of Västergötland (which has had duchesses before).
* 1979: his son [Carl Philip](/wiki/Carl_Philip "Carl Philip") was born, and Carl Gustaf created him Duke of Värmland (which has had dukes before).
* 1982: his daughter [Madeleine](/wiki/Princess_Madeleine,_Duchess_of_H%C3%A4lsingland_and_G%C3%A4strikland "Princess Madeleine, Duchess of Hälsingland and Gästrikland") was born, and Carl Gustaf created a new duchy for her as Duchess of Hälsingland and Gästrikland.
* 1983: his paternal uncle [Sigvard](/wiki/Sigvard_Bernadotte "Sigvard Bernadotte"), since 1934 no longer Prince of Sweden because of a non-royal marriage to a German woman, supported by legal experts[[61]](#cite_note-62) announced his own title as *Prince Sigvard Bernadotte*, 18 years later clearly citing a great-uncle [Prince Oscar Bernadotte](/wiki/Prince_Oscar_Bernadotte "Prince Oscar Bernadotte")'s title as his main precedent;[[62]](#cite_note-63) however, Sigvard died in 2002 with Carl Gustaf never having responded to his uncle's statement, and with the [Royal Court of Sweden](/wiki/Royal_Court_of_Sweden "Royal Court of Sweden") consistently refusing to honor it.
* 2003: his paternal grandfather's first cousin [Carl](/wiki/Prince_Carl_Bernadotte "Prince Carl Bernadotte") died, and Carl Gustaf formally recognized his Belgian title by allowing *[Prince](/wiki/Prince_Bernadotte "Prince Bernadotte") Carl Bernadotte* on the gravestone at the [Royal Cemetery](/wiki/Kungliga_begravningsplatsen "Kungliga begravningsplatsen") which is owned by the king; in 2014 he did the same there, allowing Carl's widow's name as *Princess [Kristine](/wiki/Princess_Kristine_Bernadotte "Princess Kristine Bernadotte") Bernadotte* when she died.
* 2010: his daughter Victoria married a non-royal Swede whom Carl Gustaf created a *Royal Highness* Prince of Sweden and (with her title) Duke of Västergötland.
* 2012: his granddaughter [Estelle](/wiki/Princess_Estelle,_Duchess_of_%C3%96sterg%C3%B6tland "Princess Estelle, Duchess of Östergötland") was born and created Duchess of Östergötland (which has had duchesses before).
* 2013: his daughter Madeleine married a non-royal British American who declined Swedish citizenship, and Carl Gustaf gave him the special courtesy title of *Herr* (with a capital h).
* 2014: his granddaughter Leonore was born and created Duchess of Gotland (which also previously has been a duchy).
* 2015: his son Carl Philip married a non-royal Swede whom Carl Gustaf created a *Royal Highness* Princess of Sweden and (with the son's title) Duchess of Värmland.
* 2015: his grandson Nicolas was born, and Carl Gustaf created a new duchy for him as Duke of Ångermanland.
* 2016: his grandson [Oscar](/wiki/Prince_Oscar,_Duke_of_Sk%C3%A5ne "Prince Oscar, Duke of Skåne") was born and created Duke of Scania (which has had dukes before).
* 2016: his grandson Alexander was born (later that year) and created Duke of Södermanland (which has had dukes before).
* 2017: his grandson Gabriel was born and created Duke of Dalarna (which has had dukes before).
* 2018: his granddaughter Adrienne was born, and Carl Gustaf created a new duchy for her as Duchess of Blekinge.
* 2019: Carl Gustaf issued a statement rescinding the [royal status](/wiki/Royal_highness "Royal highness") of his grandchildren Leonore, Nicolas, Alexander, Gabriel and Adrienne in an effort to more strictly associate Swedish royalty to the office of the head of state; the five are still to be styled as princes/princesses and dukes/duchesses of their provinces, and they remain in the line of succession to the throne.[[63]](#cite_note-64)[[64]](#cite_note-65)[[65]](#cite_note-66)
* 2021: his grandson Julian was born and created Duke of Halland (which has had dukes before) with the same standing of 2019 as his elder brothers.
* 2025: his granddaughter Ines was born and created Duchess of Västerbotten with the same standing of 2019 as her elder brothers.

## Titles, styles, honours and arms

Main article: [Style of the Swedish sovereign](/wiki/Style_of_the_Swedish_sovereign "Style of the Swedish sovereign")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/Royal_Monogram_of_King_Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden.svg/120px-Royal_Monogram_of_King_Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Royal_Monogram_of_King_Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden.svg)

Royal monogram

Carl XVI Gustaf ended the centuries-old traditional style "King of Sweden, the [Goths](/wiki/Geat "Geat") and the [Wends](/wiki/Wends "Wends")", instead choosing the simpler title "King of Sweden" (*Sveriges konung*).[[66]](#cite_note-67)[[13]](#cite_note-:2-13) His [personal motto](/wiki/Royal_mottos_of_Swedish_monarchs "Royal mottos of Swedish monarchs") is "For Sweden – with the times" (*För Sverige – i tiden*).[[67]](#cite_note-mottoenglish-68)[[68]](#cite_note-mottoswedish-69)

### Regnal name

There have been only ten historical kings of Sweden named "Charles" ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Carl*). The numeral "sixteen" stems from an [erroneous genealogy that includes fictitious kings](/wiki/Historia_de_omnibus_Gothorum_Sueonumque_regibus "Historia de omnibus Gothorum Sueonumque regibus"), created by 16th-century writer [Johannes Magnus](/wiki/Johannes_Magnus "Johannes Magnus").[[69]](#cite_note-70)

### Arms

On his creation as Duke of Jämtland, Carl XVI Gustaf was granted an achievement of arms which featured the arms of Jämtland in base (these arms can be seen on his [stallplate](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/stallplate "wikt:stallplate") as knight of the Danish [Order of the Elephant](/wiki/Order_of_the_Elephant "Order of the Elephant") at [Frederiksborg Palace](/wiki/Frederiksborg_Palace "Frederiksborg Palace")). Since his accession to the throne, he has used the [greater coat of arms of Sweden](/wiki/Greater_coat_of_arms_of_Sweden "Greater coat of arms of Sweden") although he is still associated with the ducal title of Jämtland he held as a prince.
